faqs.question: How much does squirrel removal cost in East Elmhurst?
faqs.answer: Squirrel removal in East Elmhurst typically costs between $900 and $2,500, depending on the severity of the infestation, number of entry points, extent of attic damage, and whether babies are present. This includes humane trapping, exclusion work, entry point sealing, and sanitation. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How quickly can you respond to a squirrel problem in East Elmhurst?
faqs.answer: We offer same-day and next-day service throughout East Elmhurst and surrounding Queens neighborhoods. Squirrel emergencies—such as animals trapped in living spaces or active chewing on electrical wiring—receive priority scheduling. Most initial inspections can be completed within 24 hours of your call.

faqs.question: What damage can squirrels cause to East Elmhurst homes?
faqs.answer: Squirrels commonly damage East Elmhurst homes by chewing through roof soffits, fascia boards, and attic vents to gain entry. Once inside, they gnaw on electrical wiring (creating fire hazards), tear insulation for nesting, contaminate areas with urine and feces, and create noise disturbances. They may also damage siding, gutters, and wooden trim on the exterior of your property.

faqs.question: Do you use humane squirrel removal methods?
faqs.answer: Yes, we exclusively use humane, ethical squirrel removal techniques in compliance with New York wildlife regulations. Our methods include live trapping and relocation, one-way exclusion doors that allow squirrels to exit but not re-enter, and thorough sealing of entry points. We never use poisons or lethal methods, and we take special care when baby squirrels are present to reunite families before exclusion.
